title: UDP socket operations — sendmsg, recvmsg, active/passive
purpose: UDP is a connectionless protocol used for DNS resolution, logging, metrics, and real-time applications. Unlike TCP, UDP operations are message-oriented: each send/recv handles a complete datagram with source/destination address. io_uring supports sendmsg and recvmsg natively. This brief provides UDP socket BIFs with the same active/passive mode pattern as TCP (B-108), delivering {udp, Socket, IP, Port, Data} messages in active mode.
task: Implement UDP open, close, send, recv BIFs using the CompletionRing. Support both active and passive receive modes. Use sendmsg/recvmsg for address-included datagram operations.

requirements.title: udp_open BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:udp_open/1 or erlang:udp_open/2 is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL create a UDP socket, bind to the specified port, and return an FdResource.
acceptance: erlang:udp_open/1 with port (0 for ephemeral), erlang:udp_open/2 with options
Test: open UDP socket, verify FdResource and bound port

requirements.title: udp_send and udp_recv BIFs
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:udp_send/4 is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL send a datagram to the specified address. WHEN erlang:udp_recv/2 is called in passive mode, THE SYSTEM SHALL suspend until a datagram arrives and return {ok, {Address, Port, Data}}.
acceptance: erlang:udp_send/4 with args (Socket, Host, Port, Data) — sends datagram
erlang:udp_recv/2 with args (Socket, Length) — receives datagram in passive mode
Send uses sendmsg via the ring, returns ok or {error, Reason}
Recv uses recvmsg via the ring, process suspends, returns {ok, {Address, Port, Binary}}
Length = 0 means receive any size datagram (up to 65535 bytes)
Test: send datagram to self (loopback), recv it, verify content and source address

requirements.title: UDP active mode
requirements.spec: WHEN a UDP socket is set to active mode, THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver incoming datagrams as {udp, Socket, IP, Port, Data} messages to the controlling process.
acceptance: Active mode uses the same background read loop pattern as TCP (B-108 R2)
Message format: {udp, Socket, IP, InPort, Data} where IP is a 4-tuple of integers
Active-once: deliver one datagram, switch to passive
Test: open socket active, send datagram from another socket, verify message delivered

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement multicast — future enhancement
SHALL NOT implement IPv6 — IPv4 first
SHALL NOT implement connected UDP sockets (connect+send) — use sendmsg directly
verification: cargo test passes with UDP send/recv loopback
Large datagram (>MTU) handling: sendmsg returns error for oversized packets
Multiple UDP sockets can operate concurrently
